# Error Handling Checklist: Claude Code Marketplace Installation Path

**Purpose**: Validate DOC-003 requirement quality for concise Claude install, verification, lifecycle, and recovery-path handling without absorbing DOC-008 troubleshooting depth.
**Created**: 2026-06-14
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Does the spec require an ordered install path that covers add marketplace, install SpecKit Pro, reload plugins, and verify the installed surface? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-004]
- [x] CHK002 Are basic recovery requirements defined for an incorrect or stale Racecraft marketplace entry before users are sent to deeper troubleshooting? [Resolved, Spec §FR-022, Spec §FR-023, Plan §Structure Decision]
- [x] CHK003 Are basic recovery requirements defined for a missing SpecKit Pro plugin or plugin-not-found install result? [Resolved, Spec §FR-022, Spec §FR-023, Plan §Structure Decision]
- [x] CHK004 Are basic recovery requirements defined for failed verification when `/plugin`, `/speckit-pro:speckit-status`, or `/speckit-pro:speckit-coach` does not show the expected installed surface? [Resolved, Spec §FR-022, Spec §FR-023, Plan §Structure Decision]
- [x] CHK005 Are basic recovery requirements defined for failed or inconclusive update, remove, uninstall, and reinstall lifecycle actions? [Resolved, Spec §FR-022, Spec §FR-023, Plan §Structure Decision]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Are expected successful verification signals required in plain language so users can distinguish success from error states? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005]
- [x] CHK007 Does each recovery path define the point where DOC-003 stops and routes the user to troubleshooting instead of expanding into a matrix? [Resolved, Spec §FR-023, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK008 Are Codex-specific install or verification failures explicitly routed away from the Claude page to the Codex page or later DOC-008 troubleshooting work? [Resolved, Spec §FR-024, Plan §Structure Decision]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK009 Do error-handling requirements stay consistent with the docs-only scope by prohibiting plugin runtime behavior, generated payload, version, and release automation changes? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK010 Do lifecycle requirements distinguish uninstalling SpecKit Pro from removing the Racecraft marketplace before describing recovery behavior? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK011 Do recovery requirements avoid unsupported claims about sandboxing, hook isolation, harmlessness, blocking behavior, or managed-marketplace safety guarantees? [Consistency, Spec §FR-019, Spec §FR-020]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK012 Can a reviewer objectively verify that lifecycle and recovery commands are source-backed by official Claude Code docs or repository-controlled plugin sources? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002, Spec §FR-010]
- [x] CHK013 Can a reviewer objectively verify that DOC-003 remains a basic install and recovery page rather than a rollback, cache-depth, or incident-response playbook? [Measurability, Spec §FR-021, Plan §Structure Decision]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK014 Are already-added marketplace, stale plugin list, pre-install inspection, uninstall-with-marketplace-retained, and generated-path distinction edge cases represented? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK015 Are wrong-marketplace, missing-plugin, failed-verification, failed-update, failed-remove, and failed-reinstall scenarios all represented as concise recovery cases? [Resolved, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-022, Spec §SC-008]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK016 Does the plan identify official Claude Code plugin, settings, and hook docs as the authority for platform behavior while using repository files for SpecKit Pro specifics? [Dependency, Plan §Technical Context, Research §Decision: Use official Claude Code docs plus repository source/generated payload citations]
- [x] CHK017 Are assumptions explicit that full troubleshooting, rollback, cache-depth, and incident response content belong outside DOC-003 unless needed for safe basic install/update/remove flow? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-021]

## Remediation Notes

- Resolved CHK002, CHK003, CHK004, CHK005, and CHK015 by adding `spec.md` FR-022, expanded edge cases, SC-008, and `plan.md` structure guidance for concise wrong-marketplace, stale-listing, missing-plugin, failed-verification, update, uninstall, marketplace-removal, and reinstall recovery cases.
- Resolved CHK007 by adding `spec.md` FR-023 plus plan constraints that stop DOC-003 after a documented basic check or one clean retry and route deeper diagnosis to DOC-008.
- Resolved CHK008 by adding `spec.md` FR-024 and plan guidance that Codex-specific install, custom-agent, cache, sandbox, approval, and runtime failures route to `/install/codex/` or DOC-008.

## Re-run Verification

- [x] CHK018 Do the updated spec and plan now define concise basic recovery for incorrect marketplace source and stale marketplace listing cases? [Resolved Verification, Spec §FR-022, Plan §Structure Decision]
- [x] CHK019 Do the updated spec and plan now define concise basic recovery for missing `speckit-pro` plugin and plugin-not-found install cases? [Resolved Verification, Spec §FR-022, Plan §Structure Decision]
- [x] CHK020 Do the updated spec and plan now define concise basic recovery for failed `/plugin`, `/speckit-pro:speckit-status`, and `/speckit-pro:speckit-coach` verification cases? [Resolved Verification, Spec §FR-022, Spec §FR-023]
- [x] CHK021 Do the updated spec and plan now define concise basic recovery for failed update, uninstall, marketplace removal, and reinstall cases? [Resolved Verification, Spec §FR-022, Spec §SC-008]
- [x] CHK022 Do the updated spec and plan now define when DOC-003 stops and routes to DOC-008 troubleshooting instead of expanding into rollback, cache-depth, or incident-response matrices? [Resolved Verification, Spec §FR-023, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK023 Do the updated spec and plan now route Codex-specific failures to `/install/codex/` or DOC-008 without adding Codex recovery commands to the Claude route? [Resolved Verification, Spec §FR-024, Plan §Structure Decision]
- [x] CHK024 Do the updated spec and plan preserve the docs-only scope while adding recovery requirements? [Resolved Verification, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-022, Plan §Constraints]
